mapping.a_element: Matrix Product States (MPS) representation for ground state calculation
mapping.b_element: Density Matrix Renormalization Group (DMRG) method for ground state calculation

mapping.a_element: factorization procedure for long products of MPS matrices to reduce computational effort
mapping.b_element: deformation parameter λ altering interaction strength in the Hamiltonian

mapping.a_element: periodic boundary conditions in 1D lattice models
mapping.b_element: non-uniform (deformed) nearest-neighbor interactions in 1D lattice models
exponential decay of spin correlation functions with increasing λ

brief_justification: While both papers use DMRG/MPS for ground state calculations in 1D spin chains, Paper A focuses on algorithmic efficiency improvements for periodic boundary conditions via matrix factorization, whereas Paper B introduces a hyperbolic deformation of the Hamiltonian itself to study spatial non-uniformity and boundary effects. The core mechanisms (matrix factorization vs. Hamiltonian deformation) are distinct.
